evals.prompt: Build a FastAPI app with SQLModel for a simple todo list. Requirements: Users (id, email, name) and Todos (id, title, completed, user_id). Set up relationships so a User has many Todos. Include create/read endpoints for both. Use session dependency injection. Use the SQLModel multiple-models pattern (Create/Read/Table).
evals.expected_output: Complete FastAPI app with SQLModel models, relationships, FastAPI routes, and session DI
expectations: Uses SQLModel base class for all models
Uses table=True on the table model
Uses Relationship() for the User->Todos relationship
Uses the multiple-models inheritance pattern (Create schema, Read schema, Table model sharing a base)
Uses get_session() FastAPI dependency generator with Session(engine)
Uses select() from sqlmodel (not sqlalchemy's select)
Uses session.exec() (not session.execute())
Uses model_validate() when converting Create schema to Table model
Uses session.add() + session.commit() + session.refresh() for create
Uses ForeignKey pattern via Field(foreign_key=...)

evals.prompt: I have a SQLAlchemy+Pydantic setup with separate models. Help me refactor it to SQLModel to reduce duplication. I have a User table with id, email, name and a Pydantic UserCreate + UserRead. Show the refactored code.
evals.expected_output: Refactored code showing SQLModel unification of Pydantic and SQLAlchemy models
expectations: Single SQLModel class hierarchy replaces separate Pydantic + SQLAlchemy classes
Uses inheritance: UserBase -> UserCreate, UserRead, User(table=True)
No duplicate field declarations between create/read/table models
Uses Field(default=None, primary_key=True) for auto-increment id
Uses sqlmodel_select or select() from sqlmodel

evals.prompt: How do I set up a many-to-many relationship in SQLModel between BlogPosts and Tags? Each post can have multiple tags, each tag can be on multiple posts. Show the complete model definitions including the link table.
evals.expected_output: Complete many-to-many SQLModel relationship setup with link model
expectations: Defines a link/junction table model with table=True
Link table has two foreign keys as composite primary key
Uses Relationship() with link_model parameter on both sides
Uses back_populates on both relationships
Uses string forward references or TYPE_CHECKING for circular imports

evals.prompt: I'm building a SQLModel app and my async session isn't working. Show me the correct way to set up AsyncSession with SQLModel, create async engine, and run a simple select query. Also explain the difference between Session and AsyncSession in SQLModel.
evals.expected_output: Correct async SQLModel session setup with engine, AsyncSession, and query pattern
expectations: Imports AsyncSession from sqlmodel.ext.asyncio.session
Uses create_async_engine with an async driver (e.g., aiosqlite, asyncpg)
Uses async with AsyncSession(engine) as session
Uses await session.exec(statement) (not session.execute)
Explains that AsyncSession delegates to sync Session via greenlet_spawn
Does not incorrectly suggest mixing sync and async engines
